fieldResolution.detail: Field resolution is graded by the base harness, not the critic slice.

reasoning.detail: The critic fired 10 real bedrock calls, one per candidate finding.

outcomes.label: A · pseudoreplication (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The naive cell-level p-value of 2.35e-7 collapses to a non-significant honestP of 0.775 when aggregated to four donor-level replicates, showing the claimed effect vanishes entirely at the proper unit of analysis.

outcomes.label: A · double dipping (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The held-out AUC of 0.58 drops substantially from the discovery AUC of 0.61, and zero of four markers retain discriminative power on the held-out split, indicating the marker program does not generalize beyond the data used to define it.

outcomes.label: A · fragility, Effector (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The Effector cluster appeared in zero of ten resolution settings, meaning it never formed as a discrete cluster anywhere in the sweep, which is worse than appearing in a narrow window and indicates the claimed distinct cell state has no stable clustering support.

outcomes.label: A · confounding (genuine)
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 1 indicates perfect collinearity between condition and the technical variable (lane), and the rank-deficient design confirms the two effects cannot be separated statistically.

outcomes.label: B · pseudoreplication (genuine, renamed columns)
outcomes.justification: The naive cell-level p-value of 1.3e-06 collapses to a clearly non-significant honestP of 0.72 when properly aggregated to 6 patient-level replicates, with a high ICC of 0.61 indicating substantial within-patient correlation that inflated the naive test; this is textbook pseudoreplication where millions of non-independent cells masquerade as the sample size.

outcomes.label: B · confounding (genuine, renamed columns)
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 1.0 indicates perfect collinearity between treatment and batch, and the rank-deficient design confirms that these effects cannot be separated statistically.

outcomes.label: C · pseudoreplication (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The honest replicate-level p-value of 4.8e-06 is highly significant, far below any conventional threshold, so the effect survives proper pseudobulk aggregation and this is not pseudoreplication.

outcomes.label: C · double dipping (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The held-out AUC is 1, identical to the discovery AUC, meaning all 4 markers perfectly separate the Rare state even on data unseen during clustering, so marker separation did not collapse.

outcomes.label: C · confounding (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 0.033 indicates near-zero association between condition and batch, the design is full rank, and the variables are explicitly separable, so there is no technical confounding to flag.

outcomes.label: C · double dipping (underpowered split)
outcomes.justification: The held-out split contains only 14 cells, which is too small to reliably estimate AUC for a subcluster comparison; the drop from 0.74 to 0.59 could easily reflect sampling noise rather than true marker collapse.

selfTests.name: rubber-stamp critic is caught
selfTests.detail: A rubber-stamp critic failed to veto the over-fires and downgrade the underpowered split, so the harness rejects it.

selfTests.name: a failed critic call fails safe toward showing
selfTests.detail: When the critic call throws, genuine findings stay flagged (shown by default), never hidden.
